society.definition: All the people living together in a country or area.

bolster.definition: To support or make something stronger.
bolster.example: Kind words can bolster someone's confidence.

crisis.definition: A very difficult or dangerous time when things could get much worse.
crisis.example: The hospital is in crisis because there are too many patients.

section.definition: A separate part of a book, document, or area.
section.example: Please read the next section of the book.

refer.definition: To talk about or mention something.
refer.example: She often refers to her childhood memories.

instilling.definition: Gradually putting a feeling or idea into someone's mind.
instilling.example: Parents play a role in instilling good values.

security.definition: The feeling of being safe and protected.
security.example: Children need a sense of security at home.

emphasizing.definition: Giving special importance to something when speaking or writing.
emphasizing.example: The teacher kept emphasizing the importance of reading.

attempt.definition: To try to do something, especially something difficult.
attempt.example: She made an attempt to learn a new language.

repair.definition: To fix something that is broken or damaged.
repair.example: It takes time to repair a broken friendship.

rupture.definition: A break or tear in something; a sudden end to a good relationship.
rupture.example: The argument caused a rupture in their friendship.

guarantees.definition: Promises that something will definitely happen.
guarantees.example: There are no guarantees of success in life.

deprived.definition: Not having the things that are necessary for a comfortable life.
deprived.example: Some children grow up in deprived areas.

result.definition: Something that happens because of something else.
result.example: Hard work can result in great success.

ideal.example: This is an ideal place to raise children.

protect.definition: To keep someone or something safe from harm.
protect.example: Parents want to protect their children.

minimize.definition: To make something as small or unimportant as possible.
minimize.example: We should minimize waste at home.

potential.definition: Possible in the future but not yet happening.

likelihood.definition: The chance that something will happen.
likelihood.example: There is a strong likelihood of rain today.

indicators.definition: Signs that show what something is like or how it is changing.
indicators.example: Happiness is one of the indicators of good health.

bond.definition: A strong feeling of connection between people.
bond.example: The bond between mother and child is very strong.

pack.definition: A group of animals that live and hunt together.

tribes.definition: Groups of people who live together and share the same culture.
tribes.example: Ancient tribes lived by hunting and gathering.

millennia.example: Humans have used fire for millennia.

wired.definition: Naturally built or designed to do something.
wired.example: Humans are wired to seek connection.

survive.definition: To continue to live, especially after a difficult situation.

species.definition: A group of living things that are the same kind.

rewarding.definition: Giving a feeling of satisfaction or pleasure.
rewarding.example: Teaching can be a very rewarding job.

capacity.definition: The ability to do or feel something.
capacity.example: She has a great capacity for kindness.

alongside.example: I sat alongside my friend during the movie.

experience.definition: Something that happens to you that affects how you feel or think.

moods.definition: The way someone feels at a particular time.

physically.definition: Related to the body, not the mind.

emotionally.example: She was emotionally affected by the movie.

addition.example: In addition to reading, she likes painting.

mutual.definition: Shared between two or more people.
mutual.example: They have mutual respect for each other.

influence.definition: The power to change how someone thinks or acts.
influence.example: Parents have a big influence on their children.

obvious.definition: Easy to see or understand; clear.
obvious.example: It was obvious that she was tired.

affect.definition: To change or have an effect on something.
affect.example: The weather can affect your mood.

unique.definition: Being the only one of its kind; special.
unique.example: Every person has a unique personality.

relationship.definition: The way people are connected to and feel about each other.

unconsciously.definition: Without being aware of it; automatically.
unconsciously.example: She unconsciously started humming a song.

infants.example: Infants need constant care and attention.

parenting.definition: The activity of raising and caring for children.
parenting.example: Parenting requires a lot of patience.

communication.definition: The sharing of information or feelings between people.
communication.example: Good communication is key in any relationship.

desirability.definition: The quality of being wanted or wished for.
desirability.example: The desirability of this plan is clear.

dialogue.definition: A conversation between two or more people.

collaborative.definition: Done by working together with others.
collaborative.example: The project was a collaborative effort.

relevant.definition: Connected to what is being talked about; important to the topic.

mental.definition: Related to the mind and thinking.
mental.example: Mental health is just as important as physical health.

conditions.definition: The things that must exist for something to happen.
conditions.example: Good conditions are needed for plants to grow.

course.definition: A direction or path of action.
course.example: We need to decide on the best course of action.

probable.definition: Likely to happen or be true.
probable.example: It is probable that it will rain.
